content.text: Template literal types use the same backtick syntax as JavaScript template strings, but at the type level. They concatenate string literal types into new string literal types. When a union appears inside a template literal, TypeScript
it — computing the cross-product of all union members to produce a new union of string literals.

attrs.alt: Template literal type composition: Color union × Size union creates a 9-member cross-product of string literals

content.text: TypeScript ships four intrinsic string manipulation types that work inside template literals. They are implemented at the compiler level (not as TypeScript code) for performance:
to extract sub-parts of a string type. This is the type-level equivalent of a regex capture group.
Template Literal Types with Mapped Types
Combining template literals with mapped types generates derived keys. This pattern appears in store libraries, event bus implementations, and form builder utilities.
CSS Class Union from Design Tokens

Template literal types can reference themselves recursively. This enables parsing string patterns of arbitrary depth — joining, splitting, or validating dot-notation paths are the most common use cases.
Combining recursive template literal paths with conditional types enables a
deep-get utility — the return type is automatically inferred from the path string.

A common Angular pattern is a typed event bus or service that emits strongly-typed events. Template literal types enable naming events with a consistent prefix scheme and mapping them to payload types:
Template literal types can enforce string format constraints at the type level, catching malformed strings at compile time:
string Does Not Narrow to a Specific Literal

content: Template literal types use backtick syntax at the type level to build new string literal types by concatenation.
Unions inside template positions are cross-producted — every combination becomes a union member.

content.text: inside a template literal extracts sub-strings — the type-level equivalent of a regex capture group.

content: Combine with mapped types and key remapping to generate typed method names, event handlers, store action names, and CSS classes.
In Angular, template literal types power typed route parameters, design-token class unions, and action type strings.
